POL-395.title: IMPLEMENT: Existing-repo operator interview for adoption
POL-395.body:
## Objective

Add a Foreman-led operator interview to existing-repo adoption that captures operator context separately from discovered evidence and makes adoption plans cite both, with approval gates before broad mutation.

## Context

Implements the design from ANALYZE POL-391 (see `smartdocs/raw/pol-391-existing-repo-operator-interview-design.md`). Reuses existing `adoption-plan.ts` evidence model and `adopt-approve.ts` gating. Depends on POL-392.

## Goal

An adoption flow where operator answers are stored separately, plans cite evidence + operator context, and no discovered doc is promoted above raw without an operator signal and approval.

## Scope

* src/cli/adopt-command.ts
* src/cli/adoption-context.ts
* src/cli/adoption-plan.ts
* src/cli/adopt-approve.ts
* src/cli/adopt-instructions.ts
* src/cli/adopt-smartdocs.ts

## Acceptance Criteria

* Operator answers persist to `.polaris/adoption/operator-context.json`, separate from the scan inventory.
* Adoption plan items carry evidence_refs, operator_refs, and a routing field.
* No doc routes above raw/review-required on discovery alone.
* Gates fire before doc movement, instruction-file, graph-root, and route changes.

## Validation

* npm run build
* npx vitest run src/cli/adopt-command.test.ts src/cli/adoption-plan.test.ts

## Ordering

Depends on POL-392 (Foreman assignment + bootstrap handoff).

## Non-goals

* Do not mutate SmartDocs lifecycle semantics.
* Do not weaken existing approval gates.

POL-402.title: IMPLEMENT: Operator-answer model stored separately from evidence
POL-402.body:
## Objective

Add an operator-answer model persisted separately from the discovered scan inventory.

## Context

First child of POL-395 (design: `smartdocs/raw/pol-391-existing-repo-operator-interview-design.md`). Keeps operator claims distinct from `RepoScanInventory`.

## Goal

A typed operator-context model and store writing to `.polaris/adoption/operator-context.json`.

## Scope

* src/cli/adoption-context.ts

## Acceptance Criteria

* `operator-context.json` round-trips through typed helpers.
* The file is written separately from the scan inventory (no merge).
* Fields include trusted_docs, stale_docs, never_touch, priority_systems, instruction_file_intent.

## Validation

* npm run build
* npx vitest run src/cli/adoption-context

## Ordering

* None (first in cluster).

## Non-goals

* Do not modify the scan inventory model.

POL-403.title: IMPLEMENT: Foreman adoption interview phase in adopt-command
POL-403.body:
## Objective

Add an `interview` phase to `adopt-command.ts`, seeded from scan-inventory gaps, that captures operator answers.

## Context

Second child of POL-395. Inserts between `scan` and `consolidate` phases; consumes the child-1 model.

## Goal

`polaris adopt` runs a Foreman-led interview seeded from inventory gaps and persists answers via the operator-context store.

## Scope

* src/cli/adopt-command.ts

## Acceptance Criteria

* A new `interview` phase exists in the AdoptPhase union and runs after scan.
* Questions are seeded from untrusted docs, ambiguous source roots, instruction files, and canonical-folder candidates.
* Answers persist via child 1; `--resume` continues.

## Validation

* npm run build
* npx vitest run src/cli/adopt-command.test.ts

## Ordering

* Depends on child 1 (operator-answer model).

## Non-goals

* Do not change doc routing yet (next child).

POL-404.title: IMPLEMENT: Adoption plan cites evidence and operator answers with routing
POL-404.body:
## Objective

Extend adoption plan items to carry evidence_refs, operator_refs, and a routing field so location alone never confers doctrine trust.

## Context

Third child of POL-395. Extends `adoption-plan.ts`; routing ∈ {raw, candidate, hold, review-required}.

## Goal

Plan items cite both evidence and operator answers, defaulting discovered docs to raw/review-required unless an operator signal promotes them.

## Scope

* src/cli/adoption-plan.ts
* src/cli/adopt-smartdocs.ts

## Acceptance Criteria

* Each plan item has evidence_refs, operator_refs, and routing.
* No doc routes above raw/review-required without an operator signal.
* Routing is honored by the smartdocs migration path.

## Validation

* npm run build
* npx vitest run src/cli/adoption-plan.test.ts

## Ordering

* Depends on child 2 (interview phase).

## Non-goals

* Do not mutate SmartDocs lifecycle semantics beyond routing.

POL-405.title: IMPLEMENT: Approval gates before doc/instruction/graph-root/route changes
POL-405.body:
## Objective

Add distinct approval gates before document movement, instruction-file changes, graph-root changes, and route scaffolding during adoption.

## Context

Fourth child of POL-395. Extends `adopt-approve.ts`; reuses `promptApproval`.

## Goal

Each broad-mutation category previews its diff and requires explicit approval before applying.

## Scope

* src/cli/adopt-approve.ts
* src/cli/adopt-instructions.ts
* src/cli/adopt-command.ts

## Acceptance Criteria

* Separate gates fire for doc movement, instruction-file edits, graph-root changes, and route scaffolding.
* Non-interactive runs without a supplied context file stop before mutation.
* Approvals are logged via existing adoption approval telemetry.

## Validation

* npm run build
* npx vitest run src/cli/adopt-command.test.ts

## Ordering

* Depends on child 3 (plan routing).

## Non-goals

* Do not weaken or remove existing gates.
